question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction object with an arbitrary payerAccountId and inject it as an inner transaction into a batch they do not own, because addInnerTransaction() only checks this.type == TransactionType.ATOMIC_BATCH.getProtoId() without verifying the inner transaction's batchKey matches any authorized signer, causing unauthorized fund transfers attributed to the spoofed payer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a forged parentConsensusTimestamp matching a legitimate ATOMIC_BATCH transaction, causing addInnerTransaction() to register their transaction as an authorized inner transaction of a batch they never signed, thereby executing operations under the batch owner's authority?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user set nonce=0 and batchKey to a non-null value on a crafted transaction, triggering the SqlEntityListener batch-parent lookup path and inserting their transaction into an existing batch's innerTransactions list without the batch owner's approval, leading to unauthorized execution of the inner transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of batchKey ownership verification in addInnerTransaction() to append their own payerAccountId and validStartNs into a victim's innerTransactions list, causing the mirror node to report the attacker's transaction as an authorized inner transaction of the victim's atomic batch?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction with type=ATOMIC_BATCH and then call addInnerTransaction() with a crafted inner transaction whose payerAccountId belongs to a victim account, since there is no signer verification in addInnerTransaction(), resulting in the victim's account being recorded as the payer of an unauthorized inner transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user race-condition the batch parent lookup in SqlEntityListener by submitting a crafted inner transaction with a parentConsensusTimestamp that collides with a legitimate batch, causing addInnerTransaction() to be called on the wrong batch parent and inserting an unauthorized inner transaction without the original batch owner's signature?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with batchKey set to an arbitrary byte array and nonce=0, bypassing the null-check guard in SqlEntityListener (batchKey != null && nonce == 0), so that addInnerTransaction() is invoked on a batch they do not control, authorizing their transaction without the batch owner's consent?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that addInnerTransaction() stores only transaction.getPayerAccountId().getId() and transaction.getValidStartNs() without any cryptographic binding to the batch's batchKey, allowing them to inject a fake inner transaction reference that points to a victim's account as payer, causing unauthorized debit attribution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple transactions with the same parentConsensusTimestamp as a legitimate ATOMIC_BATCH, causing addInnerTransaction() to be called repeatedly and flooding the innerTransactions list with unauthorized payer/validStartNs pairs, effectively forging a larger batch than the owner authorized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where type is set to TransactionType.ATOMIC_BATCH.getProtoId() but the transaction is not a real atomic batch, then call addInnerTransaction() to add arbitrary inner transactions, since the only guard is a type integer check with no cryptographic proof of batch ownership?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted transactionHash byte array and a spoofed payerAccountId in a Transaction object, causing toTransactionHash() to build a TransactionHash that maps a legitimate hash to an attacker-controlled payer, allowing the attacker to claim ownership of transactions they never signed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user set transactionHash to a collision value matching a victim's known transaction hash while setting payerAccountId to their own account, causing toTransactionHash() to produce a TransactionHash that overwrites the victim's hash-to-payer mapping, enabling the attacker to intercept or deny the victim's transaction lookup?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a null transactionHash, causing toTransactionHash() to produce a TransactionHash with a null hash field that passes or fails hashIsValid() in an unexpected way, potentially allowing a transaction with no cryptographic proof to be persisted as authorized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user set payerAccountId to a null EntityId in a Transaction, causing toTransactionHash() to call payerAccountId.getId() on a null reference and throw an exception that disrupts batch processing, or alternatively to store a zero-value payer that bypasses ownership checks downstream?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isNew() always returns true to force the persistence layer to always INSERT rather than UPDATE a Transaction, allowing them to inject a duplicate transaction with a different payerAccountId at the same consensusTimestamp, effectively replacing the authorized payer with an attacker-controlled one?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isNew() always returning true to bypass Hibernate's dirty-check and optimistic locking, submitting a Transaction with a forged consensusTimestamp that matches an existing record, causing an unauthorized INSERT that shadows the legitimate transaction's authorization data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction with a consensusTimestamp that collides with a legitimate transaction's ID (returned by getId()), causing the persistence layer to treat the attacker's transaction as the canonical record for that timestamp, overwriting the legitimate transaction's signer/owner data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user add an NftTransfer with is_approval=true to a Transaction they do not own by exploiting the absence of ownership verification in addNftTransfer(), causing the mirror node to record an NFT transfer as owner-approved when no such approval was granted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an NftTransfer with a spoofed senderAccountId into a Transaction via addNftTransfer(), since there is no check that the sender matches the transaction's payerAccountId or any authorized signer, causing an unauthorized NFT transfer to be recorded as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user call addNftTransfer() with a null NftTransfer argument to trigger a NullPointerException that corrupts the nftTransfer list state, causing subsequent legitimate NFT transfer authorization checks to fail or be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an ItemizedTransfer with an arbitrary account and amount into a Transaction via addItemizedTransfer(), since there is no signer or owner verification, causing the mirror node to record unauthorized fund movements as part of a legitimate transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of the itemizedTransfer list in addItemizedTransfer() (null check before new ArrayList<>()) to inject transfers into a transaction that previously had no itemized transfers, bypassing any downstream check that relies on the list being null to indicate no authorized transfers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user add a negative-amount ItemizedTransfer targeting a victim's account via addItemizedTransfer() without any ownership check, causing the mirror node to record an unauthorized debit from the victim's account as part of a transaction the victim never signed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: batchKey] Can an unprivileged external user set the batchKey field to a byte array that matches a known public key of a legitimate batch operator, causing the mirror node to treat their transaction as authorized by that operator's batch key without any cryptographic signature verification in the Transaction domain object?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: batchKey] Can an unprivileged external user set batchKey to a non-null value on a non-ATOMIC_BATCH transaction type, causing the SqlEntityListener batch-parent lookup to be triggered (batchKey != null && nonce == 0) and associating their transaction with a batch they do not own, effectively authorizing it under a foreign batch key?
